title: hieracium patens subsp. costae
intro: Hieracium patens subsp. costae, also known as Hawkweed, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, Hawkweed has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce inflammation and improve overall health. Hawkweed can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal medicine to treat a range of ailments, including colds, fever, and digestive issues.
